content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed branch delta against ca4d23414de261e5b7d9716c8c608569045ab18d. The two-part fix is correctly implemented: (A) build_checkpoint_tree now drops tracked-but-gitignored paths via ls-files -i -c --exclude-per-directory=.gitignore + update-index --force-remove, and preserves parent-version blobs for assume-unchanged paths via ls-files -v -s -z piped into update-index --index-info; (B) commit 70eabf73 stops tracking the result symlink. Tests exercise the gitignored case, assume-unchanged preservation, explicit non-use of .git/info/exclude and core.excludesFile, and non-UTF-8 paths on Unix. Verified git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard is empty on current HEAD, pre-existing checkpoint tests are unaffected (they introduce no .gitignore), and the ls-files -v -s -z format parsing matches git's actual record layout. Minor theoretical gaps exist (assume_unchanged_index_entries's nth(1) tab split truncates paths containing literal tabs but the index_info blob it feeds to update-index is still the full correct bytes, and remove_paths_from_index passes every path as argv which could hit ARG_MAX on repos with thousands of tracked+gitignored files) but neither produces a real-world correctness or safety problem given the filter logic and expected path counts. No amendments.

## Amendments

None.
